dominantJob.reasoning: Because the screen displays numerous empty state containers and post-generation actions before the user has even started a session, which violates the principle of having one clear primary action — compared to ChatGPT's focused empty state — this scores 4/10.
violations: Three post-generation action buttons ('Save report', etc.)
Suggestion pills visible before a prompt

visibleReasoning.reasoning: Because the UI explicitly carves out space for sources and structured reasoning (even in the empty state), which aligns with building trust — compared to Perplexity's inline citations — this scores 7/10.

speedBehavior.reasoning: Because the interface pre-renders empty response containers rather than progressively revealing them upon generation, which clutters the initial view — compared to Linear's clean initial states — this scores 5/10.

qualityDiscipline.reasoning: Because there are logical inconsistencies in the copy and button states (showing post-action buttons in an empty state), which violates quality discipline — compared to Vercel's strict state management — this scores 6/10.
papercuts: Post-generation buttons visible in empty state

contextCompounding.reasoning: Because the interface explicitly shows the active lens and allows persona selection to guide the AI, which supports context compounding — compared to Notion AI's tone selection — this scores 7/10.

chromeCollapse.reasoning: Because the UI relies heavily on borders and boxes to separate every single element, which violates the principle of chrome collapse — compared to Linear's whitespace-driven hierarchy — this scores 3/10.

components.name: Input Field
components.beforeInteraction: Empty with placeholder text 'Continue the live session...'
components.afterInteraction: Clears or shows the submitted prompt
components.reasoning: Essential for starting the chat session, but placeholder copy needs fixing.

components.name: PENDING Answer Boxes
components.reasoning: Should be removed from the empty state and progressively revealed only when content is generating.

components.name: Bottom Action Buttons
components.reasoning: Unnecessary visual noise in the empty state; should only appear after a report is generated.

components.name: Persona Pills
components.reasoning: Useful for context, but adds clutter. Consider moving to a dropdown or removing borders.

components.name: Sources Panel
components.reasoning: Crucial for trust, though the empty state text could be more subtle.

competitorComparison: Compared to ChatGPT, NodeBench's chat surface is overly cluttered in its empty state, showing pre-rendered response containers and post-generation actions before a prompt is even entered. However, NodeBench excels in its explicit structural reasoning, carving out dedicated space for sources and specific analytical lenses (like 'What it is' and 'Why it matters'), which provides more guided intelligence than a blank text box.
topIssues: Pre-rendered 'PENDING' boxes clutter the empty state and distract from the primary input.
Post-generation actions ('Save report', 'Share link') are visible before any content exists.
Excessive use of borders on almost every UI element creates visual noise.
Conflicting copy between the header ('Start a live session') and input placeholder ('Continue the live session...').
The primary input field is pushed down the page by empty containers.
recommendations: Remove the four 'PENDING' boxes from the initial empty state; reveal them progressively as the AI generates the response.
Hide the bottom action buttons ('Save report', etc.) until a response has been fully generated.
Remove borders from the suggestion and persona pills, using subtle background fills instead to reduce chrome.
Update the input placeholder to match the initial state (e.g., 'Ask a question to start...').
Center the input field vertically in the empty state, similar to ChatGPT, to focus the user on the primary action.
